repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 is nearby but targets joint f-divergence/outcome-misspecification policy-value intervals, not CH residual-R2 policy-regret support

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022: sharp OVB bounds for linear causal functionals via the Riesz representer, including policy effects; this subsumes the CH/Cauchy-Schwarz support-bound algebra

KallusZhou2021 and JinXuZhengZhou2025 give minimax/robust policy learning under MSM; ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022 supplies the CH-style linear-functional bias geometry, but not this Tikhonov minimax policy formula
none; verified KallusZhou2021, ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019, and AdjahoChristensen2025 do not state CH boundary latent environments or the proposed bridge limits

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The CH support-function upper bound is essentially the published Riesz-representer OVB bound of Chernozhukov, Cinelli, Newey, Sharma, and Syrgkanis (2022) specialized to a policy contrast.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 | Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal omits ChernozhukovCinelliNeweySharmaSyrgkanis2022, the closest CH/Riesz functional prior art for policy-effect OVB bounds.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the proposal states a genuinely sharp new PartialID bound or an iff frontier beyond combining CH/Riesz OVB geometry with Kallus-Zhou-style minimax policy learning.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 uses the marginal sensitivity model for IPW sensitivity; the proposal overstates it as formalizing/introducing the MSM odds-ratio relaxation, which is usually credited to Tan (2006).

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 | Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The CH budget is imposed on the factual residual Y(A)-m0(A,X), but the value V_Q depends on counterfactual Y(a); without a restriction on potential-outcome/Riesz residual bias, Q can match P while changing counterfactual means outside the displayed support bound.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Assumption 'Sharp support and smooth minimax face' assumes the upper bound is sharp for every local contrast, which is a load-bearing part of the flagship claim rather than a regularity condition.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Chernozhukov, Cinelli, Newey, Sharma, and Syrgkanis (2022) as a primary comparator and explicitly state whether Theorem 1 is only a calibration lemma rather than a novelty claim.
Replace the factual-residual-only CH restriction with a potential-outcome or Riesz-representer bias restriction that actually controls V_Q(alpha)-V_Q(beta).
Remove the circular sharp-support assumption and move sharpness into Conjecture 2, with explicit boundary-environment construction conditions.

To meet the flagship floor, strengthen Conjecture 1/2 into either a new sharp PartialID bound with a strict improvement over the Riesz/CH conservative envelope, or an iff frontier versus named MSM policy estimators with a computable threshold.

recommended_next_step: Revise before Stage 0: fix the counterfactual-residual restriction, add the missing Riesz-OVB prior art, correct the MSM attribution, and sharpen the flagship claim beyond a field-tier synthesis.
